1.q: The ethylene receptor family... which act as negative regulators of the downstream signaling cascade... RhETR3 degradation is a key intersection hub for the ethylene signalling-mediated regulation of salt stress.

2.q: RhTSPO accelerates the degradation of RhETR3. N. benthamiana leaves expressing RhETR3-Flag and RhTSPO-GFP were infiltrated with no (0) or 200 mM NaCl for 6 hours before sample collection.

3.q: The improved salt tolerance of RhETR3-silenced rose plants is partly due to the increased expression of ACC SYNTHASE1 (ACS1) and ACS2, which results in an increase in ethylene production.

5.q: Reduced transcript levels of these osmolyte-related genes may result in poor osmotic adjustment ability... in RhNAC3-silenced petals.
